```asp
:- launches(Name, 1, C), C != "Techtrin".
```

```asp
% Define the constants
presenters("Alexander"; "Gerard"; "Inez"; "Mable").
days("May 10th"; "May 11th"; "May 12th"; "May 13th").
topics("global warming"; "nitrogen usage"; "sulfur oxide"; "wind power").

% Each presenter has exactly one day and one topic
{talk(P, D, T) : days(D), topics(T)} = 1 :- presenters(P).

% Each day has exactly one presenter and topic
```
```asp
:- days(D), #count{P, T : talk(P, D, T)} != 1.

% Each topic is used exactly once
:- topics(T), #count{P, D : talk(P, D, T)} != 1.

% No duplicate assignments in any category
:- talk(P1, D1, T1), talk(P2, D2, T2), P1 != P2, D1 = D2, T1 = T2.
:- talk(P1, D1, T1), talk(P2, D2, T2), P1 = P2, D1 != D2, T1 = T2.
:- talk(P1, D1, T1), talk(P2, D2, T2), P1 = P2, D1 = D2, T1 != T2.

% 1. The sulfur oxide expert is scheduled 1 day after Gerard
:- talk("Gerard", D1, _), talk(P, D2, "sulfur oxide"), D2 != D1 + 1.

% 2. The nitrogen usage expert is scheduled 1 day before Alexander
:- talk("Alexander", D1, _), talk(P, D2, "nitrogen usage"), D2 != D1 - 1.

% 3. Inez will discuss global warming
talk("Inez", D, "global warming") :- presenters("Inez"), days(D).

% 4. Mable is scheduled 2 days after the sulfur oxide expert
:- talk(P, D1, "sulfur oxide"), talk("Mable", D2, _), D2 != D1 + 2.

% Additional constraints to prevent impossible situations
:- talk("Gerard", "May 13th", _).  % Gerard cannot be on May 13th
:- talk("Alexander", "May 10th", _).  % Alexander cannot be on May 10th
:- talk(P, "May 12th", "sulfur oxide").  % Sulfur oxide cannot be on May 12th
:- talk(P, "May 13th", "sulfur oxide").  % Sulfur oxide cannot be on May 13th
```

```asp
% Hope paid less than Delia
:- order(Hope, _, Ph), order(Delia, _, Pd), Ph >= Pd.
```

```asp
% The 115 ft tall waterfall is in Nigeria or Tibet
```

```asp
names("Clara"; "Fannie"; "Hannah"; "Kara").
points("181 points"; "184 points"; "187 points"; "190 points").
orders(2; 4; 6; 7).

% Each dancer has exactly one score and one order
dancer(N, P, O) :- names(N), points(P), orders(O).

% Each name appears exactly once
:- names(N), #count{P,O : dancer(N,P,O)} != 1.

% Each point value appears exactly once

% Each order appears exactly once
:- orders(O), #count{N,P : dancer(N,P,O)} != 1.

% 1. The person who danced seventh scored somewhat higher than the dancer who performed fourth
:- dancer(_, P7, 7), dancer(_, P4, 4), P7 <= P4.

% 2. The dancer who performed second scored 3 points lower than the dancer who performed fourth
:- dancer(_, P2, 2), dancer(_, P4, 4), P2 != P4 - 3.

% 3. Kara scored somewhat lower than Fannie
:- dancer("Kara", Pk, _), dancer("Fannie", Pf, _), Pk >= Pf.
```

```asp
% We forbid cases where this is not true
```
